steps.title: Simplify and interpret the exponent
steps.content: Rewrite: $\frac{6}{4}t = 1.5t$. Since $t$ is in years, the coefficient 1.5 represents the number of 4% growth periods per year.

steps.title: Find the period in months
steps.content:
If growth of 4% occurs every $n$ months, then in 12 months (1 year), there are $\frac{12}{n}$ periods. This must equal 1.5:
$$\frac{12}{n} = 1.5$$
$$n = \frac{12}{1.5} = 8$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
